Implement the following plan:

# Fix Gemini thought_signature round-tripping (#375) and schema validation (#793)

## Context

**#375**: Gemini 3.x models return `thought_signature` on each tool call in their OpenAI-compat
API responses. When Moltis replays tool calls in subsequent turns, this field must be present or
Gemini rejects with HTTP 400. Currently `ToolCall` only carries `id`, `name`, `arguments` -- the
signature is silently dropped at parse time. This affects both direct Gemini API and OpenRouter routing.

**#793**: When OpenRouter routes to Google AI Studio (not Vertex), tool schemas fail validation
because `required` arrays reference properties that Google AI Studio considers undefined. The
existing `PruneOrphanedRequiredTransform` (added for #747) doesn't catch all edge cases -- likely
properties with type-only or description-only schemas that AI Studio treats as undefined.

These interact: users can't work around #375 via OpenRouter because #793 blocks that path.

## Approach: provider-metadata passthrough on ToolCall

Add an opaque `metadata` field to `ToolCall` that preserves any provider-specific fields (like
`thought_signature`) through the full round-trip: API response -> typed struct -> persistence ->
deserialization -> next API request.

Use an allowlist of known metadata keys to avoid bloating session storage. Start with
`["thought_signature"]`; extensible for future provider-specific fields.

## Steps

### 1. Add `metadata` to core types

**`crates/agents/src/model.rs`** -- `ToolCall` struct (line 610):
```rust
pub struct ToolCall {
    pub id: String,
    pub name: String,
    pub arguments: serde_json::Value,
    /// Provider-specific opaque metadata to round-trip (e.g. Gemini thought_signature).
    pub metadata: Option<serde_json::Map<String, serde_json::Value>>,
}
```
Add `metadata: None` to all ~80 construction sites. Compiler-driven: every site will fail to compile until updated.

**`crates/agents/src/model.rs`** -- `StreamEvent::ToolCallStart` (line 447):
```rust
ToolCallStart {
    id: String,
    name: String,
    index: usize,
    metadata: Option<serde_json::Map<String, serde_json::Value>>,
},
```
Update ~39 match sites across 29 files to destructure or ignore the new field.

**`crates/sessions/src/message.rs`** -- `PersistedToolCall` (line 176):
```rust
pub struct PersistedToolCall {
    pub id: String,
    #[serde(rename = "type")]
    pub call_type: String,
    pub function: PersistedFunction,
    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub metadata: Option<serde_json::Map<String, serde_json::Value>>,
}
```
`serde(default)` ensures backward compat with existing stored sessions.

### 2. Extract metadata from Gemini API responses

**`crates/providers/src/openai_compat/provider.rs`**:

Add helper (near `parse_tool_calls`, line 263):
```rust
const TOOL_CALL_METADATA_KEYS: &[&str] = &["thought_signature"];

fn extract_tool_call_metadata(tc: &serde_json::Value) -> Option<serde_json::Map<String, serde_json::Value>> {
    let obj = tc.as_object()?;
    let meta: serde_json::Map<_, _> = TOOL_CALL_METADATA_KEYS.iter()
        .filter_map(|&k| obj.get(k).map(|v| (k.to_string(), v.clone())))
        .collect();
    if meta.is_empty() { None } else { Some(meta) }
}
```

**Non-streaming** (`parse_tool_calls`, line 263): call `extract_tool_call_metadata(tc)` and set on `ToolCall`.

**Streaming** (`process_openai_sse_line`, ~line 670): extract metadata from the first SSE chunk of
each tool call (where `id` and `function.name` appear). Add `tool_call_metadata: HashMap<usize, Option<Map>>>`
to `StreamingToolState`. Emit in `ToolCallStart`.

### 3. Serialize metadata back to API requests

**`crates/agents/src/model.rs`** -- `to_openai_value()` (line 200):
When building `tool_calls` JSON array, merge metadata fields into each tool call object:
```rust
if let Some(ref meta) = tc.metadata {
    if let Some(obj) = tc_val.as_object_mut() {
        for (k, v) in meta { obj.insert(k.clone(), v.clone()); }
    }
}
```

### 4. Deserialize metadata from persisted JSON

**`crates/agents/src/model.rs`** -- `values_to_chat_messages()` (line 361):
When parsing tool_calls from stored JSON, call `extract_tool_call_metadata(tc)` to
recover metadata that was persisted alongside standard fields.

### 5. Update persistence layer

**`crates/chat/src/service/types.rs`** -- `build_persisted_tool_call()` (line 112):
Add `metadata` parameter, pass through to `PersistedToolCall`.

**`build_tool_call_assistant_message()` (line 129)**: propagate metadata.

### 6. Strengthen schema normalization for #793

**`crates/providers/src/openai_compat/schema_normalization.rs`** -- `PruneOrphanedRequiredTransform`:

Tighten the "defined property" check. Currently treats any non-empty, non-boolean schema as defined.
Add a `has_usable_type` check -- a property must have `type`, `enum`, `anyOf`, `oneOf`, `allOf`, or
`$ref` to be considered defined:
```rust
fn has_usable_type(v: &serde_json::Value) -> bool {
    let Some(obj) = v.as_object() else { return false };
    ["type", "enum", "anyOf", "oneOf", "allOf", "$ref"]
        .iter().any(|k| obj.contains_key(*k))
}
```

Also add a post-normalization `EnsurePropertyTypeTransform` that infers `"type": "string"` for
properties in `required` that have a description but no type info. This is safer than pruning the
property because it preserves the parameter name for the LLM.

### 7. Tests

#### Unit tests (Rust)

- **ToolCall metadata round-trip** (`model.rs`): serialize via `to_openai_value()`, verify
  `thought_signature` on tool_call JSON; deserialize via `values_to_chat_messages()`, verify
  metadata preserved.
- **PersistedToolCall backward compat** (`message.rs`): deserialize without `metadata` -> `None`;
  serialize with `metadata: None` -> field absent; serialize with metadata -> field present.
- **Streaming metadata extraction** (`openai_compat/tests`): simulate SSE chunk with
  `thought_signature`, verify `ToolCallStart` carries it.
- **Schema normalization** (`schema_normalization.rs`): property without type in `required` gets
  pruned or type-inferred; nested `items.required` handled.

#### Integration tests

- **Update `gemini_integration.rs:multi_turn_tool_use`**: remove the `thought_signature`-expected-
  failure path. Multi-turn tool use should now succeed.

#### E2E tests (Playwright)

- **New spec `crates/web/ui/e2e/specs/tool-call-metadata.spec.js`**: exercise multi-turn tool call
  via the web UI with a mock/fixture provider that returns `thought_signature`. Verify the
  conversation continues past the tool call without errors.
- **New spec `crates/web/ui/e2e/specs/schema-validation.spec.js`**: verify tool schemas sent to the
  API pass Google AI Studio's stricter validation (mock the schema endpoint, assert `required`
  entries match defined properties).

## Files to modify

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `crates/agents/src/model.rs` | `ToolCall` struct, `StreamEvent`, `to_openai_value()`, `values_to_chat_messages()` |
| `crates/providers/src/openai_compat/provider.rs` | `parse_tool_calls()`, `process_openai_sse_line()`, `StreamingToolState` |
| `crates/providers/src/openai_compat/schema_normalization.rs` | Strengthen `PruneOrphanedRequiredTransform`, add type inference |
| `crates/sessions/src/message.rs` | `PersistedToolCall` metadata field |
| `crates/chat/src/service/types.rs` | `build_persisted_tool_call()`, `build_tool_call_assistant_message()` |
| `crates/agents/src/runner/streaming.rs` | Propagate metadata from `ToolCallStart` to `ToolCall` |
| ~80 files constructing `ToolCall { ... }` | Add `metadata: None` |
| ~29 files matching on `ToolCallStart` | Add `metadata` field or `..` |
| `crates/providers/tests/gemini_integration.rs` | Update multi-turn test to expect success |
| `crates/web/ui/e2e/specs/tool-call-metadata.spec.js` | New e2e test |
| `crates/web/ui/e2e/specs/schema-validation.spec.js` | New e2e test |

## Commit plan

1. `feat(agents): add metadata field to ToolCall and StreamEvent for provider round-tripping`
2. `feat(providers): extract and replay Gemini thought_signature in tool calls`
3. `fix(providers): strengthen schema required-pruning for Google AI Studio (#793)`
4. `test: add e2e tests for tool call metadata round-trip and schema validation`

## Verification

1. `cargo build` -- all 80+ sites compile with `metadata: None`
2. `just test` -- all existing tests pass
3. `cargo test --test gemini_integration -- --ignored multi_turn` (with `GEMINI_API_KEY`) -- multi-turn succeeds
4. `cd crates/web/ui && npx playwright test e2e/specs/tool-call-metadata.spec.js`
5. `cd crates/web/ui && npx playwright test e2e/specs/schema-validation.spec.js`
6. `just lint` / `just format-check` -- clean


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/penso/.claude/projects/-Users-penso--superset-worktrees-moltis-adorable-protocol/234fffa1-9204-44ac-a6fe-8c073dacd052.jsonl
